# zstack_load_balancer_listener (Resource)

Manage ZStack load balancer listeners. A listener defines the protocol and port configuration for a load balancer, routing traffic from a frontend port to backend instance ports.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `instance_port` (Number) The port on the backend instances that receives forwarded traffic.
- `load_balancer_port` (Number) The port on the load balancer (frontend port) that receives traffic.
- `load_balancer_uuid` (String) The UUID of the load balancer this listener belongs to.
- `name` (String) The name of the load balancer listener.
- `protocol` (String) The protocol for the listener: tcp, udp, http, or https.

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the load balancer listener.
- `security_policy_type` (String) The security policy type for HTTPS listeners (e.g., tls_cipher_policy_default).

### Read-Only

- `server_group_uuid` (String) The UUID of the server group associated with this listener.
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the load balancer listener.
